page: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The architecture provides 32 vCPUs with a single thread per core, backed by 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache. It includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in large-block memory bandwidth for both read and write operations, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is rated as strong, as is its performance in software compilation using Clang. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier and strong categories, respectively. However, single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average range, and large LLM inference prompt processing drops to the bottom tier.

The D32pls_v6 offers a compute-focused profile with a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high parallel processing power without massive memory footprints. The absence of local storage means storage costs and performance depend entirely on attached premium remote volumes. The ARM64 architecture lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets, which limits its suitability for specialized vector math or heavy on-CPU matrix operations, as reflected in the poor large-scale LLM inference benchmarks.

This server configuration is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, static web serving, and database operations that fit within the 64.0 GB memory limit. It is also appropriate for Redis deployments and medium-scale text or image processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but supports premium remote storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU execution and software compilation capabilities. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM inference is limited, with prompt processing for 70B models falling into the bottom tier. This compute-oriented, low-memory configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads, making it suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, Redis deployments, and CI/CD compilation pipelines that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios or local disk storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 64 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered, compute-focused virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for parallelized workloads without high memory overhead.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark results
No bundled local storage or graphics processing units
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and compilation pipelines
